hypothesis: 3D sliding window attention (STA-style) via flex_attention should provide speedup by limiting each token's attention to a local spatiotemporal neighborhood (±5 frames, ±4 spatial), exploiting video's strong temporal locality while preserving quality.

3D sliding window attention (STA-style) via flex_attention should provide speedup by limiting each token's attention to a local spatiotemporal neighborhood (±5 frames, ±4 spatial), exploiting video's strong temporal locality while preserving quality.

PISA piecewise sparse attention selects top-k blocks per query block based on block-level scores, achieving sparse attention without any training. At density=0.15, should provide significant speedup with better quality than fixed-window approaches since it's data-dependent.

VMoBA mixture-of-block attention selects top-k relevant KV chunks per query using block-level scoring. With chunk_size=3 (temporal frames), topk=3, should provide speedup via flash-attn varlen backend while preserving quality through data-dependent selection.

VMoBA mixture-of-block attention selects top-k relevant KV chunks per query using block-level scoring. With chunk_size=3 (temporal frames), topk=3, should provide speedup via flash-attn varlen backend while preserving quality through data-dependent selection.

hypothesis: PISA with Triton allocator fix — previous run (exp_0005) silently fell back to dense due to Triton 3.4.0 allocator error. Now with explicit TorchCUDAAllocator, PISA's block-level sparse attention should actually execute. At density=0.15, expects ~6.7x theoretical sparsity.

VMoBA with higher topk=5 (vs default topk=3). With 7 temporal chunks and topk=5, each query attends 71% of KV (vs 43% at topk=3). Should improve quality (lower FID/LPIPS) at cost of some speedup. Baseline VMoBA got 3.64x/FID=203/LPIPS=0.66.

hypothesis: VMoBA with lower simsum_threshold=0.10 (vs default 0.25). Lower threshold means fewer chunks pass the relevance filter, making attention sparser. Should increase speedup at cost of quality. Previous: threshold=0.25 gave 3.64x/FID=203/LPIPS=0.66.

VMoBA with lower simsum_threshold=0.10 (vs default 0.25). Lower threshold means fewer chunks pass the relevance filter, making attention sparser. Should increase speedup at cost of quality. Previous: threshold=0.25 gave 3.64x/FID=203/LPIPS=0.66.
NABLA/STA with wider 3D windows (wT=10,wH=10,wW=10) vs default (5,4,4). Larger receptive field should improve quality (FID/LPIPS) at cost of some speedup. Default gave 4.23x/FID=367/LPIPS=0.88.

Hybrid dense→VMoBA: dense SDPA for first 10/50 denoising steps (global context), VMoBA sparse for remaining 40 steps. Should improve quality over pure VMoBA (FID~203, LPIPS~0.66) while retaining most of the 3.64x speedup (expect ~2.5-3x).

Hybrid dense→VMoBA with only 5 dense steps (vs 10 in #13). Should be faster than #13 (3.43x) while hopefully retaining much of the quality gain (FID=157.63). Testing if 5 dense steps is enough for global context.
Hybrid dense→VMoBA with 20 dense steps (40% dense, 60% sparse). Completing the dense_steps curve: 0/5/10/20. Expect highest quality of hybrids but slowest (~2.5x speedup).

hypothesis: Full-mode evaluation of best hybrid config (dense_steps=10). Fast mode showed FID=157.63, LPIPS=0.4494, composite=0.2104, speedup=3.43x. Full mode adds SSIM, PSNR, ImageReward, HPSv2 on 25 prompts.

Hybrid dense→NABLA: dense for first 10 steps, NABLA 3D sliding window for remaining 40. NABLA was fastest (4.23x) so hybrid should be faster than hybrid VMoBA (3.43x). Pure NABLA had FID=367; with 10 dense steps expect FID improvement similar to VMoBA hybrid.

Hybrid dense→NABLA with wide windows (10,10,10). Default windows (5,4,4) + 10 dense steps barely helped (FID=364 vs 367). Wider windows should preserve more global context. Pure NABLA wide got FID=301 at 2.85x; hybrid should improve quality further.

Layer-selective: dense on boundary layers (0-4, 25-29), VMoBA on middle (5-24). 20/30 layers sparse. Tests whether spatial locality in layer-space matters — boundary layers handle low-level features, middle layers handle semantics where sparse is sufficient.

Combined timestep + layer selective: dense everywhere for steps 0-9, then dense on layers 0-4/25-29 and VMoBA on layers 5-24 for steps 10-49. More dense computation than pure timestep hybrid (3.43x) so expect slower but potentially better quality. Tests if layer selectivity adds value on top of timestep selection.

Combined with wider boundary (8 layers from each end vs 5). Only 14/30 middle layers use VMoBA. #20 with boundary=5 got composite=0.3024 at 3.17x. Wider boundary means more dense = better quality but slower.

Combined with narrow boundary (3 layers from each end). 24/30 middle layers use VMoBA. Boundary=5 got composite=0.30 at 3.17x, boundary=8 got 0.35 at 3.04x. Boundary=3 should be faster (~3.3x?) with moderate quality. Mapping the curve.

SLA sparse-linear attention with 30% key retention (topk=0.3). methods.yaml warns 0% negligible weights (SLA predicts 45%). Testing empirically. Zero-initialized proj_l means linear attention branch contributes nothing without fine-tuning — effectively just sparse block attention.

Hybrid dense→SLA: dense for first 10 steps, SLA sparse for remaining 40. Pure SLA got 3.76x/composite=0.167. Hybrid VMoBA(10) improved composite from 0.099→0.210. Expect similar improvement for SLA, potentially reaching composite ~0.25-0.30 at ~3.5x speedup.

Combined timestep + layer selective SLA (boundary=5). Hybrid SLA(10) got composite=0.319 at 3.50x — already competitive with combined VMoBA(b=5) at 0.302/3.17x. Adding layer selectivity should improve quality further. With VMoBA, combined(b=5) improved from hybrid 0.210→0.302. Expect similar gains for SLA.

Combined SLA with wider boundary (8 layers). Combined SLA(b=5) got NEW BEST composite=0.364 at 3.20x. Combined VMoBA(b=8) got 0.354 at 3.04x. With VMoBA, b=5→b=8 improved composite from 0.302→0.354. Expect SLA b=8 to reach ~0.40+ at ~3.0x.
